nodes.name: ISO/IEC 42001:2023
nodes.description: Information technology - Artificial intelligence - Management system

nodes.name: Improvement
nodes.description: Continual improvement

nodes.full_description: The organization shall ensure that its responsible approach to the development and use of AI systems considers their customer expectations and needs.
nodes.name: Customers

nodes.description: Consequences to environment sustainability, economic, government, health and safety, or norms, traditions, culture and values
Consequences affecting the physical or psychological well-being of individuals, or universal human rights
The AI systems that are developed, provided or used by the organization
Human resources, i.e. people with the necessary expertise (e.g. for the development, sales, training, operation and maintenance of the AI system)
System and computing resources (e.g. hardware to develop and run AI models, storage for data and tooling resources)
Tooling resources (e.g. AI algorithms, models or tools)
Data resources, i.e. data used at any stage in the AI system life cycle
Relevant authorities, including policymakers and regulators
AI subjects, including data subjects and other subjects
AI partners, including AI system integrators and data providers
AI customers, including AI users
AI producers, including AI developers, AI designers, AI operators, AI testers and evaluators, AI deployers, AI human factor professionals, domain experts, AI impact assessors
AI providers, including AI platform providers, AI product or service providers

nodes.full_description: The organization shall establish a process to ensure that its usage of services, products or materials provided by suppliers aligns with the organization's approach to the responsible development and use of AI systems.
nodes.name: Suppliers

nodes.full_description: The organization shall ensure that responsibilities within their AI system life cycle are allocated between the organization, its partners, suppliers, customers and third parties.
nodes.name: Allocating responsibilities

nodes.full_description: The organization shall ensure that the AI system is used according to the intended uses of the AI system and its accompanying documentation.
nodes.name: Intended use of the AI system

nodes.full_description: The organization shall identify and document objectives to guide the responsible use of AI systems.
nodes.name: Objectives for responsible use of AI system

nodes.full_description: The organization shall define and document the processes for the responsible use of AI systems.
nodes.name: Processes for responsible use of AI systems

nodes.category_name: Information for interested parties of AI systems
nodes.full_description: The organization shall determine and document their obligations to reporting information about the AI system to interested parties.
nodes.name: Information for interested parties

nodes.category_name: Information for interested parties of AI systems
nodes.full_description: The organization shall determine and document a plan for communicating incidents to users of the AI system.
nodes.name: Communication of incidents

nodes.category_name: Information for interested parties of AI systems
nodes.full_description: The organization shall provide capabilities for interested parties to report adverse impacts of the AI system.
nodes.name: External reporting

nodes.category_name: Information for interested parties of AI systems
nodes.full_description: The organization shall determine and provide the necessary information to users of the AI system.
nodes.name: System documentation and information for users

nodes.full_description: The organization shall define and document its criteria for selecting data preparations and the data preparation methods to be used.
nodes.name: Data preparation

nodes.full_description: The organization shall define and document a process for recording the provenance of data used in its AI systems over the life cycles of the data and the AI system.
nodes.name: Data provenance

nodes.full_description: The organization shall define and document requirements for data quality and ensure that data used to develop and operate the AI system meet those requirements.
nodes.name: Quality of data for AI systems

nodes.full_description: The organization shall determine and document details about the acquisition and selection of the data used in AI systems.
nodes.name: Acquisition of data

nodes.full_description: The organization shall define, document and implement data management processes related to the development of AI systems.
nodes.name: Data for development and enhancement of AI system

nodes.full_description: The organization shall determine at which phases of the AI system life cycle, record keeping of event logs should be enabled, but at the minimum when the AI system is in use.
nodes.name: AI system recording of event logs

nodes.full_description: The organization shall determine what AI system technical documentation is needed for each relevant category of interested parties, such as users, partners, supervisory authorities, and provide the technical documentation to them in the appropriate form.
nodes.name: AI system technical documentation

nodes.full_description: The organization shall define and document the necessary elements for the ongoing operation of the AI system. At the minimum, this should include system and performance monitoring, repairs, updates and support.
nodes.name: AI system operation and monitoring

nodes.full_description: The organization shall document a deployment plan and ensure that appropriate requirements are met prior to deployment.
nodes.name: AI system deployment

nodes.full_description: The organization shall define and document verification and validation measures for the AI system and specify criteria for their use.
nodes.name: AI system verification and validation

nodes.full_description: The organization shall document the AI system design and development based on organizational objectives, documented requirements and specification criteria.
nodes.name: Documentation of AI system design and development

nodes.full_description: The organization shall specify and document requirements for new AI systems or material enhancements to existing systems.
nodes.name: AI system requirements and specification

nodes.category_name: Management guidance for AI system development
nodes.full_description: The organization shall define and document the specific processes for the responsible design and development of the AI system.
nodes.name: Processes for responsible AI system design and development

nodes.category_name: Management guidance for AI system development
nodes.full_description: The organization shall identify and document objectives to guide the responsible development AI systems, and take those objectives into account and integrate measures to achieve them in the development life cycle.
nodes.name: Objectives for responsible development of AI system

nodes.full_description: The organization shall assess and document the potential societal impacts of their AI systems throughout their life cycle.
nodes.name: Assessing societal impacts of AI systems

nodes.full_description: The organization shall assess and document the potential impacts of AI systems to individuals or groups of individuals throughout the system's life cycle.
nodes.name: Assessing AI system impact on individuals or groups of individuals

nodes.full_description: The organization shall document the results of AI system impact assessments and retain results for a defined period.
nodes.name: Documentation of AI system impact assessments

nodes.full_description: The organization shall establish a process to assess the potential consequences for individuals or groups of individuals, or both, and societies that can result from the AI system throughout its life cycle.
nodes.name: AI system impact assessment process

nodes.full_description: As part of resource identification, the organization shall document information about the human resources and their competences utilized for the development, deployment, operation, change management, maintenance, transfer and decommissioning, as well as verification and integration of the AI system.
nodes.name: Human resources

nodes.full_description: As part of resource identification, the organization shall document information about the system and computing resources utilized for the AI system.
nodes.name: System and computing resources

nodes.full_description: As part of resource identification, the organization shall document information about the tooling resources utilized for the AI system.
nodes.name: Tooling resources

nodes.full_description: As part of resource identification, the organization shall document information about the data resources utilized for the AI system.
nodes.name: Data resources

nodes.full_description: The organization shall identify and document relevant resources required for the activities at given AI system life cycle stages and other AI-related activities relevant for the organization.
nodes.name: Resource documentation

nodes.full_description: The organization shall define and put in place a process to report concerns about the organization's role with respect to an AI system throughout its life cycle.
nodes.name: Reporting of concerns

nodes.full_description: Roles and responsibilities for AI shall be defined and allocated according to the needs of the organization.
nodes.name: AI roles and responsibilities

nodes.full_description: The AI policy shall be reviewed at planned intervals or additionally as needed to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.
nodes.name: Review of the AI policy

nodes.full_description: The organization shall determine where other policies can be affected by or apply to, the organization's objectives with respect to AI systems.
nodes.name: Alignment with other organizational policies

nodes.full_description: The organization shall document a policy for the development or use of AI systems.
nodes.name: AI policy

nodes.text: When a nonconformity occurs, the organization shall react to the nonconformity and as applicable take action to control and correct it
The organization shall continually improve the suitability, adequacy and effectiveness of the AI management system.
Top management shall review the organization's AI management system, at planned intervals, to ensure its continuing suitability, adequacy and effectiveness.
The organization shall conduct internal audits at planned intervals
The organization shall determine what needs to be monitored and measured
The organization shall perform AI system impact assessments according to 6.1.4 at planned intervals or when significant changes are proposed to occur.
The organization shall implement the AI risk treatment plan according to 6.1.3 and verify its effectiveness.
The organization shall perform AI risk assessments in accordance with 6.1.2 at planned intervals or when significant changes are proposed or occur.
The organization shall plan, implement and control the processes needed to meet requirements, and to implement the actions determined in Clause 6
The organization's AI management system shall include documented information required by this document
The organization shall determine the internal and external communications relevant to the AI management system
Persons doing work under the organization's control shall be aware of the AI policy
The organization shall determine the necessary competence of person(s) doing work under its control that affects its AI performance
The organization shall determine and provide the resources needed for the establishment, implementation, maintenance and continual improvement of the AI management system.
When the organization determines the need for changes to the AI management system, the changes shall be carried out in a planned manner.
The organization shall establish AI objectives at relevant functions and levels.

The organization shall define a process for assessing the potential consequences for individuals or groups of individuals, or both, and societies that can result from the development, provision or use of AI systems.
Taking the risk assessment results into account, the organization shall define an AI risk treatment process to select appropriate AI risk treatment options
The organization shall define and establish an AI risk assessment process

When planning for the AI management system, the organization shall consider the issues referred to in 4.1 and the requirements referred to in 4.2 and determine the risks and opportunities that need to be addressed
Top management shall ensure that the responsibilities and authorities for relevant roles are assigned and communicated within the organization.
Top management shall establish an AI policy
Top management shall demonstrate leadership and commitment with respect to the AI management system

The organization shall establish, implement, maintain, continually improve and document an AI management system, including the processes needed and their interactions, in accordance with the requirements of this document.
The organization shall determine the boundaries and applicability of the AI management system to establish its scope.
The organization shall determine the interested parties that are relevant to the AI management system
The organization shall determine external and internal issues that are relevant to its purpose and that affect its ability to achieve the intended result(s) of its AI management system.

nodes.name: Performance evaluation
nodes.description: Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation

nodes.name: Operation
nodes.description: Operational planning and control

nodes.name: Support
nodes.description: Resources and competence

nodes.name: Planning
nodes.description: Actions to address risks and opportunities

nodes.name: Leadership
nodes.description: Leadership and commitment

nodes.name: Context of the organization
nodes.description: Understanding the organization and its context

nodes.name: Third-party and customer relationships
nodes.description: To ensure that the organization understands its responsibilities and remains accountable, and risks are appropriately apportioned when third parties are involved at any stage of the AI system life cycle.

nodes.name: Use of AI systems
nodes.description: To ensure that the organization uses AI systems responsibly and per organizational policies.

nodes.name: Information for interested parties of AI systems
nodes.description: To ensure that relevant interested parties have the necessary information to understand and assess the risks and their impacts (both positive and negative).

nodes.name: Data for AI systems
nodes.description: To ensure that the organization understands the role and impacts of data in AI systems in the application and development, provision or use of AI systems throughout their life cycles.

nodes.name: AI system life cycle
nodes.description: To define the criteria and requirements for each stage of the AI system life cycle.

nodes.name: Management guidance for AI system development
nodes.description: To ensure that the organization identifies and documents objectives and implements processes for the responsible design and development of AI systems.

nodes.name: Assessing impacts of AI systems
nodes.description: To assess AI system impacts to individuals or groups of individuals, or both, and societies affected by the AI system throughout its life cycle.

nodes.name: Resources for AI systems
nodes.description: To ensure that the organization accounts for the resources (including AI system components and assets) of the AI system in order to fully understand and address risks and impacts.

nodes.name: Internal organization
nodes.description: To establish accountability within the organization to uphold its responsible approach for the implementation, operation and management of AI systems.

nodes.name: Policies related to AI
nodes.description: To provide management direction and support for AI systems according to business requirements.
